_source: ILO Future of Work LMIC adjustment + ITU Digital Development Report 2024 — Ghana automation calibration multiplier. Lower than OECD baseline because (a) infrastructure constraints slow capital substitution, (b) informal economy share is 81%, where automation pressure is structurally weaker.
rationale: Multipliers below 1.0 reflect ILO finding that Frey-Osborne overstates LMIC automation displacement by ~30-40% in low-formality, low-capital sectors. BPO and ICT remain near-baseline because they sit on global digital infrastructure.
